title: Pyrus wardii
taxonomy: Pyrus wardii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrus, which includes other pear species. The genus Pyrus is part of the tribe Pyreae within the subfamily Maloideae. Pyrus wardii is native to the Himalayan region. It is a deciduous tree or shrub species that is often cultivated for its ornamental value.
uses: Pyrus wardii, a species of pear tree native to the Himalayas, has been found to have potential medicinal uses. benefits: Pyrus wardii, also known as the Ward's pear tree, is a deciduous tree native to China. intro: Pyrus wardii, also known as the Tibetan Pear or Himalayan Pear, is a species of deciduous tree or shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The health benefits of Pyrus wardii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus wardii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
